entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00021
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00021.
The file contains 2 DIV1, 5 DIV2, 15 DIV3, 3,507 LG, 24,542 L, 884 PB, 11,066 MILESTONE, and 253 NOTE elements.
The encoded text ends three streams at Book V line 1834 although the source edition continues through line 1869.

entities.description: A public structured digital text representing the title page and most of Furnivall's three parallel witness transcriptions and notes.
entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00021
notes: The public item page identifies the 1894–1895 edition and applies No Copyright–United States.
Comparison with the scan shows that the encoding omits nearly all preliminary matter, Macaulay's note on Benoît, and the final two printed pages of the poem.

rights.basis: The U-M item page applies the No Copyright–United States rights statement.

access.accessMethod: HathiTrust bibliographic record

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Three More Parallel Texts of Chaucer's Troilus and Criseyde, from MS. L.1 in St. John's College, Cambridge; MS. No. 61 in Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; and the Harleian MS. 1239 in the British Museum, with a Note on Chaucer's Borrowings from Benoît de Sainte-More by G. C. Macaulay, Chaucer Society, First Series 87–88, 2 parts (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner; Oxford: Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, 1894–1895).
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner; Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press
bibliographic.title: Three More Parallel Texts of Chaucer's Troilus and Criseyde

dateStatements.label: publication
dateStatements.notes: The combined scan includes Furnivall's copying-and-collation note dated 13 February 1896; the exact issue history of the bound title leaf remains unresolved.

entities.description: A two-part Chaucer Society parallel-text edition whose bound title leaf dates the parts 1894–1895 and whose copying-and-collation note is dated 13 February 1896.
entities.label: Furnivall, Three More Parallel Texts, 1894–1895
notes: A. Rogers copied and collated the St John's and Corpus manuscripts; Furnivall copied the Harley manuscript.
Macaulay's separately headed note on Chaucer's borrowings from Benoît de Sainte-More is edition-level supplementary scholarship and is not an intrinsic part of Troilus and Criseyde.
The Harley 1239 column is composite where bracketed or noted supplied readings come from Additional MS 12044 or Harley MS 2392.

rights.basis: The edition was published in 1894–1895, well before the U.S. public-domain cutoff described in Copyright Office Circular 15A.
rights.component: The 1894–1895 edition, transcriptions, Furnivall and Rogers editorial work, and Macaulay note
notes: This status does not transfer to a modern scan service or manuscript photographs.

access.accessMethod: Consult in the Rare Books Reading Room by prior appointment
access.entityLabel: St John's College, Cambridge, MS L.1

access.accessMethod: Request a complete-object reading copy or high-resolution publication images from Special Collections
access.entityLabel: St John's College, Cambridge, MS L.1
access.format: Reading copy for private research or high-resolution images for publication, subject to repository response
notes: Request the complete present codex: front and rear boards, spine and edges if photographable, all flyleaves, fols. 1–128 including blank and later Testament leaves, pastedowns, stubs, and any unfoliated matter. Map Troilus and Criseyde separately to fols. 1r–119v.
Private research supply and permission for public reproduction are separate decisions.

rights.basis: St John's distinguishes private-research copies from images for publication and requires permission for reproduction in publications, websites, or public presentations.
rights.component: Any modern reading-copy or high-resolution images supplied by St John's College

access.accessMethod: Late Medieval English Scribes record with selected page and letter images
access.entityLabel: St John's College, Cambridge, MS L.1
access.format: Selected web images, not a whole-codex surrogate
notes: No explicit open-reuse license was verified, so the selected images were not cached.
The project announced migration to a University of Sheffield archive in July 2026.

dateStatements.label: manuscript production
dateStatements.notes: The Late Medieval English Scribes record gives 1425–1450; James's older catalog uses a broader fifteenth/sixteenth-century description.

entities.description: A parchment codex containing Troilus and Criseyde on fols. 1r–119v, followed by Robert Henryson's Testament of Cresseid in a later hand.
entities.label: St John's College, Cambridge, MS L.1
notes: No public whole-codex digital surrogate was found on 2026-07-11.
The online Late Medieval English Scribes record exposes selected scholarly images but no explicit open-reuse license was found, so no selected image was cached.

physicalDescription.extent: Three paper flyleaves, one parchment flyleaf, fols. 1–128, and three paper flyleaves according to Late Medieval English Scribes
notes: Troilus and Criseyde ends on fol. 119v; the later Testament of Cresseid occupies the remainder.
Acquired by St John's College in 1683; the principal scribe is unidentified.
physicalDescription.support: Parchment, with paper and parchment flyleaves

rights.basis: St John's distinguishes private-research copies from images for publication and requires permission for reproduction in publications, websites, or public presentations.
rights.component: Any modern reading-copy or high-resolution images supplied by St John's College

entities.description: A luxury parchment copy of Troilus and Criseyde, planned for more than ninety images but completed with only the full-page frontispiece on fol. 1v.
entities.label: Corpus Christi College, Cambridge, MS 61
notes: The manifest description calls this one of the best manuscripts; it is not treated here as the earliest known witness of the work.

physicalDescription.extent: 151 folios plus two; foliation a–d, i–ii, 1–151, e–h
notes: Five seven-line stanzas per page.
The poem occupies fols. 1v–150r; the painted frontispiece is on 1v, text begins on 2r, and the rubric Explicit liber Troily appears on 150r.
Fol. 150v records ownership and fol. 151r contains an English receipt.

access.accessMethod: British Library Archives and Manuscripts catalog
British Library digital item viewer
notes: The current catalog explicitly reports the digital images as unavailable; no excerpts were cached as a substitute.

access.accessMethod: Submit the British Library digitisation order form for complete-object photography
access.contact: British Library Imaging Services via the online order form; permissions@bl.uk for reproduction permission
access.format: Photography of original manuscript; scope subject to quotation and condition
notes: Request the complete present object: all 107 folios, the blank after fol. 85 and stub, both sets of flyleaves, pastedowns, boards, spine, gilt fore-edge and other edges if photographable, blanks, repairs, and the adjacent Canterbury Tales text. Map Troilus separately to fols. 1r–62v.
The service distinguishes reference images from high-quality reproduction images; public reuse requires the relevant permissions.

rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services warns that copyright may subsist and requires written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material.
rights.component: Any modern reference or reproduction images supplied through British Library Imaging Services
notes: The British Library catalog site's CC BY notice applies to site text, not automatically to manuscript images.

entities.description: A narrow parchment 'holster book' containing Troilus and Criseyde on fols. 1r–62v and selections from the Canterbury Tales on fols. 63r–106r.
entities.label: British Library Harley MS 1239
notes: Furnivall's right-hand column is not solely a transcription of this witness because explicitly marked lacunae are supplied from Additional MS 12044 and Harley MS 2392.

physicalDescription.extent: 107 folios, with three paper flyleaves at front and rear, an unfoliated blank parchment leaf after fol. 85, and a stub
notes: Four hands; the first three copy Troilus and Criseyde.
Two leaves are lost after fol. 3, removing Book I lines 379–630.
British Museum binding, gilt fore-edge; fol. 102 is torn and repaired.

rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services warns that copyright may subsist and requires written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material.
rights.component: Any modern reference or reproduction images supplied through British Library Imaging Services
notes: The British Library catalog site's CC BY notice applies to site text, not automatically to manuscript images.

access.contact: British Library Imaging Services via the online order form; permissions@bl.uk for reproduction permission
access.format: Photography of original manuscript; scope subject to quotation and condition
notes: Request the complete present object: all 113 folios, all front and rear flyleaves, bindings with Butler arms, spine and edges if photographable, pastedowns, blanks, stubs, and any unfoliated matter. Map the encoded supplied passages separately.
Reference-image supply and permission for public reproduction are separate decisions.

rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services warns that copyright may subsist and requires written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material.
rights.component: Any modern reference or reproduction images supplied through British Library Imaging Services

entities.description: An imperfect parchment witness of Troilus and Criseyde used by Furnivall to supply selected gaps in the Harley 1239 column.
entities.label: British Library Additional MS 12044

physicalDescription.extent: One medieval flyleaf and 113 folios, with two paper and one parchment flyleaves at front and one parchment and one paper flyleaf at rear
notes: Troilus and Criseyde occupies fols. 1r–113v and is imperfect at the end.
Three leaves are absent before fol. 113, removing Book V lines 1541–1750, and one leaf after fol. 113 removes lines 1826–1869.
Two scribes; green leather binding with the arms of Charles Butler.

rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services warns that copyright may subsist and requires written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material.
rights.component: Any modern reference or reproduction images supplied through British Library Imaging Services

entities.description: A complete paper-and-parchment manuscript of Troilus and Criseyde used for one supplied stanza in Furnivall's Harley 1239 column.
entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2392

physicalDescription.extent: 145 folios, with three paper and one parchment flyleaves at front and one parchment and three paper flyleaves at rear
notes: Troilus and Criseyde occupies fols. 1r–145v.
The scribe signs 'Style' on fol. 145v; the manuscript was rebound in 1964.
physicalDescription.support: Paper and parchment, with outer and inner bifolia of parchment in predominantly twelve-leaf quires

rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services warns that copyright may subsist and requires written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material.

workPortion.label: Troilus and Criseyde
notes: The bundle includes all provider canvases, including both boards, endleaves, blanks, fol. 150v ownership, fol. 151r receipt, and other non-work surfaces.
No spine or edge canvas is published.

access.provider: Parker Library, Corpus Christi College, Cambridge

rights.basis: The IIIF manifest and MODS record explicitly license the published images under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International.

notes: Required attribution: Images courtesy of The Parker Library, Corpus Christi College, Cambridge.

entities.description: A 326-canvas IIIF surrogate including inside and outside boards, front and rear endleaves, fols. 1r–151v, and blank or non-work leaves exposed by the provider.
entities.label: Parker Library digital facsimile of Corpus Christi College MS 61
notes: Representative images 1, 16, 17, 313, and 326 were visually inspected after download; they show both boards, the illuminated frontispiece, text, and the work's ending in legible full color.
The provider exposes no separate spine or edge canvas, so the local bundle is complete relative to the published IIIF object, not every physically possible surface.

next_steps: Consult U-M production documentation or report the endpoint discrepancy to the provider before repairing the source XML.
If a correction is authorized, transcribe all three final columns from the complete scan and preserve their distinct explicits.
openQuestions.question: Was the shared omission of printed pp. 295–296 and Book V lines 1835–1869 intentional in the original U-M encoding or an accidental truncation?

next_steps: Compare separately issued wrappers and title leaves for Chaucer Society First Series 87 and 88 in institutional copies.
Do not assign a post-1895 publication date solely from the dated copying note.
openQuestions.question: Does the combined title leaf in the Google scan represent the original two-part issue, a later bound issue, or a title leaf reset after Furnivall's 13 February 1896 note?

next_steps: Request a whole-object research quotation from special.collections@joh.cam.ac.uk.
Inspect the 1983 printed facsimile's leaf and exterior inventory before using it as a fallback.
openQuestions.question: Can St John's College supply or license a complete present-object digital reproduction of MS L.1, including all bindings, flyleaves, blanks, and the adjacent Testament of Cresseid?

next_steps: Submit separate complete-object imaging requests with all bindings, flyleaves, blanks, stubs, pastedowns, adjacent texts, spine, and edges specified.
Obtain written reuse terms separately from the supply quotation.
openQuestions.question: Will the British Library restore public images or quote complete-object photography for Harley MS 1239, Additional MS 12044, and Harley MS 2392?

next_steps: Establish folio crosswalks from restored public images or complete research reproductions.
Recheck bracket boundaries against the print scan before using the passage list for automated collation.
openQuestions.question: What exact manuscript folios correspond to every passage Furnivall supplies from Additional MS 12044 and Harley MS 2392?

relations.objectLabel: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00021

scope.notes: Identifiers, title-page transcription, structure, and text agree; the exact repository transfer date is not documented.

object.description: University of Michigan CME item CME00021

relations.subjectLabel: General Corpus source XML for CME00021

scope.notes: The encoding serializes each printed three-column page as version 1 St John's, version 2 Corpus, and version 3 Harley. It omits the dedication, copying-and-collation note, Macaulay's essay, publisher lists, and printed pp. 295–296 (Book V lines 1835–1869) in all three columns.

object.description: The edition title page and parallel poem through printed p. 294

subject.description: The complete surviving U-M digital text

scope.notes: A. Rogers copied and collated this column; the later Testament of Cresseid is outside the transcribed work.

scope.notes: A. Rogers copied and collated this column.

subject.description: Corpus MS 61 parallel column, version 2 in each XML book

scope.notes: Furnivall copied the Harley manuscript. The excluded intervals are separately related to the witnesses that supply them.

locators: excluding Book I lines 379–630, 890–896, and 904–973; Book III lines 953–959 and 1387–1393; Book IV lines 953–1085 and 1149–1155; Book V lines 225–231

scope.notes: Only direct, bracketed or explicitly identified supplies are modeled; comparative notes that merely cite Additional MS 12044 are not treated as source substitution.

reviewNotes: The five manuscript entities are textual sources for the edition, but only three are its principal parallel columns; Additional MS 12044 and Harley MS 2392 supply explicitly marked passages in the composite Harley 1239 column.
A complete physical-object reproduction is not the same as the work portion. Work folios/pages/canvases are mapped separately in each access or relation record.

notes: Request the complete present object: all 145 folios, all front and rear flyleaves, pastedowns, boards, spine and edges if photographable, blanks, stubs, and any unfoliated matter. Map Furnivall's supplied Book I stanza 128 separately.
The catalog says a letter of introduction is required for physical consultation; imaging feasibility is a separate service decision.

rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services warns that copyright may subsist and requires written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material.
rights.component: Any modern reference or reproduction images supplied through British Library Imaging Services

sourceLinks.accessMethod: Official U.S. Copyright Office PDF

lineage.summary: The repository XML is a local copy of University of Michigan CME item CME00021, an encoding of Frederick J. Furnivall's 1894–1895 Chaucer Society edition Three More Parallel Texts of Chaucer's Troilus and Criseyde. The edition aligns independent transcriptions of St John's College Cambridge MS L.1, Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 61, and British Library Harley MS 1239; Furnivall fills explicitly marked gaps in the Harley 1239 column from British Library Additional MS 12044 and one stanza from Harley MS 2392. The XML serializes the three printed columns as sequential witness-version divisions and is incomplete at the end: it stops at Book V line 1834 on printed page 294 and omits printed pages 295–296, lines 1835–1869, in all three streams. A complete Google Books scan of the bound edition and a complete 326-canvas Parker Library IIIF surrogate of Corpus MS 61 are cached. No public complete digital surrogate was verified for St John's L.1 or the three British Library manuscripts on 2026-07-11; complete-object photography is requestable, subject to repository terms, condition, and fees.

catalogSummary.author: Geoffrey Chaucer
catalogSummary.region: England; exact place of composition not securely established; narrative set in Troy
catalogSummary.title: Troilus and Criseyde

catalogingSubject.label: Troilus and Criseyde
catalogingSubject.scope_note: Geoffrey Chaucer's five-book Middle English poem, as represented in CME00021 by Frederick J. Furnivall's parallel transcriptions of St John's College MS L.1, Corpus Christi College MS 61, and a composite Harley MS 1239 column with explicitly supplied passages from Additional MS 12044 and Harley MS 2392. The work is distinct from those manuscript objects, Furnivall's 1894–1895 edition and Macaulay supplement, the U-M electronic encoding, and this repository's XML artifact. The XML serializes three witness versions within each book; those versions are representations of one work, not fifteen intrinsic parts.

contentParts.label: Book I
notes: Metrics aggregate the three encoded streams.
The composite Harley stream supplies lines 379–630 and 904–973 from Additional MS 12044 and lines 890–896 from Harley MS 2392.
contentParts.summary: Troilus first sees and falls in love with Criseyde; Pandarus undertakes to mediate.

contentParts.label: Book II
notes: Metrics aggregate the three encoded streams; the shared page 40 contains the Book I/II transition.
contentParts.summary: Pandarus advances the courtship and brings Troilus and Criseyde into communication.

contentParts.label: Book III
notes: Metrics aggregate the three encoded streams.
The composite Harley stream supplies lines 953–959 and 1387–1393 from Additional MS 12044.
contentParts.summary: The lovers are united and experience the poem's central period of joy.

contentParts.label: Book IV
notes: Metrics aggregate the three encoded streams.
The composite Harley stream supplies lines 953–1085 and 1149–1155 from Additional MS 12044.
contentParts.summary: Criseyde is exchanged for Antenor, separating the lovers and provoking debate over destiny and free will.

contentParts.label: Book V
notes: Metrics aggregate the three encoded streams only through line 1834.
Printed pp. 295–296, stanzas 263–267 and lines 1835–1869, including explicit endings, are absent from all three XML versions.
The composite Harley stream supplies lines 225–231 from Additional MS 12044.
contentParts.summary: Criseyde remains with the Greeks and Diomede; Troilus dies in battle and looks back on earthly love from the heavens.

assertion.notes: The Middle English Compendium's stencil gives c1385; the approximation is not normalized to an exact year.

extent.description: Five intrinsic books represented through fifteen witness-version divisions. The repository XML contains 3,507 LG elements and 24,542 L elements across all three streams: St John's L.1 1,174/8,219; Corpus MS 61 1,159/8,108; composite Harley 1239 1,174/8,215. Every stream ends at Book V line 1834, omitting edition lines 1835–1869, so these are encoding metrics rather than the stanza or line count of a complete canonical text.

formStatements.description: A five-book narrative poem predominantly in seven-line rhyme-royal stanzas (ababbcc), with embedded songs, letters, prayers, and an envoy retained as verse.

languageStatements.label: Middle English
notes: Brief Latin incipits, explicits, names, and learned quotations do not make the poem as cataloged a substantively multilingual work.
Macaulay's edition-level note quotes Old French from Benoît, but that supplement is outside the abstract work and omitted from CME00021.
The work record deliberately catalogs one poem, while the lineage record preserves three principal edition columns, two direct supply witnesses, and their material gaps.
Furnivall's printed arrangement is parallel; the XML's sequential DIV3 structure is an encoding transformation and must not be interpreted as intrinsic order.

George Campbell Macaulay's note on Chaucer's borrowings from Benoît de Sainte-More is edition-level supplementary scholarship, not a responsibility statement for the poem itself, and the XML omits it.
No first-known-attestation statement is inferred from Corpus MS 61; an earlier securely dated witness exists outside this immediate lineage.
No single dialect region is assigned to the abstract poem from this multi-witness source.

next_steps: Consult a current critical introduction devoted to the poem's dating and revision phases before adding normalized bounds.
openQuestions.question: Can the composition and revision chronology be narrowed beyond conventional c. 1385 dating without overstating disputed internal and historical evidence?

next_steps: Use reading-level manuscript and editorial analysis; do not assign the work one source witness's localization.
openQuestions.question: Which linguistic features belong to Chaucer's compositional language and which belong to the five scribal sources or Furnivall's transcription conventions?

next_steps: Keep the current extent explicitly labeled as encoding metrics.
If the XML is repaired, recompute all Book V and total metrics before changing content_structure_status.
openQuestions.question: Should work-level counts remain tied to the incomplete XML or be supplemented with independently transcribed metrics for edition lines 1835–1869?

notes: This does not assign a precise composition place or transfer any one witness's production region to the abstract poem.

scope.description: Creative retelling drawing especially on Giovanni Boccaccio's Il Filostrato
The 1894–1895 parallel-text source edition represented by CME00021
Harley MS 1239 column, including explicitly marked supplies from Additional MS 12044 and Harley MS 2392
Parallel alignment, lineation, witness notes, and edition supervision
St John's MS L.1 and Corpus Christi College MS 61 columns
St John's MS L.1 and Corpus Christi College MS 61 columns

summaries.value: A five-book Middle English narrative poem recounting Troilus's love for Criseyde during the Trojan War, their union through Pandarus, Criseyde's exchange to the Greek camp and relationship with Diomede, and Troilus's death and final vision beyond earthly mutability. Chaucer transforms Boccaccio's Il Filostrato through sustained reflection on love, agency, fidelity, Fortune, providence, and authorship.

CME00021 represents Furnivall's three-column edition as sequential XML divisions: for each of the five books, St John's L.1 is version 1, Corpus MS 61 version 2, and the composite Harley 1239 column version 3. Explicit passages in version 3 derive instead from Additional MS 12044 or Harley MS 2392. The XML also omits the final thirty-five lines and explicit ending in every stream. Counts below therefore describe an incomplete encoding's witness streams, not one canonical text.

notes: A shortened transcription of the edition's long title. It names the parallel-text source layer, not a medieval authorial title.
titles.value: Three More Parallel Texts of Chaucer's Troilus and Criseyde from MS. L.1 in St. John's College, Cambridge; MS. No. 61 in Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; and the Harleian MS. 1239 in the British Museum

work.author: Geoffrey Chaucer
